status_definitions.implemented: Available through a tested Linux-native path.
status_definitions.partial: A useful subset is available, with documented missing behavior.
status_definitions.in_progress: Implementation exists on the 0.11 development branch but is not released.
status_definitions.planned: Suitable for a Linux-native implementation but not yet present.
status_definitions.not_portable: Depends on a Windows-only provider or filesystem with no verified Linux equivalent.
status_definitions.out_of_scope: Not appropriate for the ARM64/Linux product goal.

features.upstream_feature: Create bootable drives from disk images, including compressed images
features.notes: Raw, ISOHybrid, ZIP, gzip, bzip2, XZ, LZMA, Zstandard, VHD, VHDX, QCOW2, and VMDK inputs are supported with source and target identity checks.
GPT/MBR, UEFI, x86 BIOS/CSM, FAT32, NTFS, WIM splitting, UEFI:NTFS, setup options, and driver staging are available.

features.upstream_feature: Improve Windows installation experience through OOBE parameters
features.notes: Hardware-check bypass, local/offline account, privacy, regional settings, and BitLocker controls exist; newer Rufus policy and application-removal options remain to be inventoried.
Guarded GPT/UEFI creation is available for the qualified Ubuntu casper and Debian live-boot contract; broader distribution coverage remains planned.

features.upstream_feature: Perform runtime validation of UEFI bootable media
features.notes: The 0.11 branch validates fallback loaders, PE architecture and EFI subsystem fields, bounded SBAT metadata, and optional DBX hash/certificate revocations. Boot-chain reference resolution and trusted SBAT-level comparison remain planned.

features.upstream_feature: Compute MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 checksums
features.notes: SHA-256 is implemented throughout destructive workflows; user-facing MD5, SHA-1, SHA-512, and checksum-file comparison remain planned.

features.upstream_feature: Perform bad-block checks, including fake flash-drive detection
features.notes: Windows-media workflows provide a destructive single-pass write/read option; multi-pattern tests, wraparound detection, and reusable qualification reports remain planned.

features.upstream_feature: Create VHD/DD, VHDX, and FFU images of an existing drive
features.notes: Start with identity-bound raw and compressed backups, then evaluate sparse VHD/VHDX export.
Requires a Linux-native wimlib deployment path, BCD construction, offline registry changes, and extensive physical qualification.

features.upstream_feature: Download official Microsoft Windows and UEFI Shell images
features.notes: Threshold-signed acquisition and rollback-resistant cache foundations exist, but the production catalog is deliberately disabled until offline trust metadata is provisioned.
FAT32, NTFS, and ext4 persistence are implemented. exFAT, UDF, and selected ext workflows are planned; ReFS is not portable through a verified Linux formatter.

features.upstream_feature: Create FreeDOS or MS-DOS bootable drives
features.notes: DOS boot targets are x86 BIOS environments and do not advance the primary Ubuntu ARM64 goal; FreeDOS may be reconsidered only as an explicitly cross-architecture utility.

features.upstream_feature: Read and write Full Flash Update images
features.notes: The official Windows FFU provider has no verified Linux-native equivalent; RufusArm64 continues to refuse FFU rather than claim unsafe compatibility.
GTK strings and diagnostics must be moved into a translation catalog before language packs are accepted.
The Debian package is self-contained for privileged helpers and WIM support; AppImage/portable packaging and verified updates remain planned.
